# Page 1^page=1
Name: Lisa A English | DOB: 7/25/1964 | MRN: 055656 | PCP: Jung Wook Kang Smith, MD | Legal Name: Lisa A English ED Provider Notes Signed Jun 19, 2026 .CHIEF COMPLAINT HPI Lisa A English is a 61 y.o. female who presents with inability to care for herself at home. She was discharged yesterday for severe COPD with exacerbation. She uses an electric scooter to get around and in the past friends and family been able to help her at home transition between her recliner and the scooter. She thought her son was going to be able to help care for her at home but found out upon arriving home that he has left and will not come back. Home health which plan on seeing her once a week visit her today for the 1st time and actually called the primary and said, via review of the chart, that they did not think that this was going to work out. An ambulance was called when patient came here because she states she can not care for herself and she has no one to care for her at home. She has been in her recliner since being transported back home yesterday. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS Twelve point review of systems obtained and otherwise unremarkable except as above. PAST MEDICAL HISTORY Chief Complaint Patient presents with • Failure To Thrive Past Medical History: Diagnosis Date • Anxiety 07/20/2023 • Arthritis • Bilateral ovarian cysts • Cataract 04/12/2019 • Chicken pox • COPD (chronic obstructive pulmonary disease) (HCC) • Coronary artery disease • Depression • Emphysema of lung (HCC) 04/04/2021 • GERD (gastroesophageal reflux disease) • Heart palpitations • HPV (human papilloma virus) anogenital infection 02/04/2021 ED Provider Notes by William Jones at 06/19/26 2149

# Page 11^page=11
Old charts were reviewed per EPIC EMR. Pertinent details are summarized above. All laboratory, radiologic, and EKG studies that were performed in the Emergency Department were a necessary part of the evaluation needed to exclude unstable or emergent medical conditions. Pertinent studies were reviewed as above. The patient received: The initial differential diagnosis for this patient's presentation include: Severe end-stage COPD, debility, Each of the above mentioned unique tests were ordered by myself for workup of this patient. I have reviewed the external records available to me, pertaining to this patient's care. I have reviewed the results of each unique tests mentioned above. The Independent Historian, nursing, paramedics, was used to gather history pertaining to this patient's care. My Independent Interpretation of this patient's history, exam and workup (including laboratory, radiology or ancillary testing) are most consistent with severe end-stage COPD, debility. Inability to care for self at home and poor social situation. It is not safe to discharge her back to her current living situation. I do feel she needs likely a skilled nursing facility. Will admit to hospitalist for case management consultation and PT consultation likely placement.
